# RAVEN — Borrower Verification for Community Banks

RAVEN is automated borrower verification software for community and regional banks. The product name is RAVEN; the company URL is https://reportraven.tech. RAVEN stands for Real-time Aggregation and Verification Engine.

## What RAVEN does

A loan officer enters a borrower's email or phone in the RAVEN dashboard. The borrower receives a secure link that works on any device, with no app to download. They enter their SSN and connect their primary bank account through Plaid Link — the same bank-connection interface used by Venmo, Cash App, and most major fintechs. The entire borrower interaction takes about 5 minutes.

Behind that interaction, RAVEN runs seven verification providers in parallel. The results come back cross-referenced and structured into a single report:

- **Identity and KYC** — Socure RiskOS performs identity validation, fraud risk scoring, synthetic identity detection, and OFAC/PEP/global watchlist screening. Output is a granular risk profile with reason codes, not a pass/fail checkbox.
- **Income and assets** — Plaid connects to the borrower's primary financial institution and returns categorized transaction history, identified income streams, current balances, and existing liabilities. Income is verified at the source, not from borrower-provided screenshots.
- **Employment** — Truework returns employer-verified salary, title, employment dates, and active status. Verification is asynchronous and typically completes in 1 to 3 days.
- **Property** — Melissa and ATTOM together provide automated valuation, tax assessment, ownership records, property characteristics, and comparable sales.
- **Contact and demographics** — Melissa and FullContact provide verified phone and email, and demographic indicators useful for fair lending analysis.

Every data point in the report is sourced, timestamped, and given a confidence indicator. When sources agree (Plaid income matches Truework salary, for example), confidence increases. When they disagree, the discrepancy is flagged before the file reaches the loan committee.

## Who RAVEN is for

Community banks, regional banks, and federal savings associations that originate mortgages, HELOCs, commercial real estate, and small business loans. RAVEN is designed for institutions that want to compete with fintech-grade verification speed without replacing their existing loan origination system or running an 18-month implementation project.

The product is particularly aligned with banks in the Southeast and in the $200M–$10B asset range, though it works for any size institution that runs borrower verification.

## How RAVEN deploys

RAVEN works as a standalone platform accessible through a web dashboard from day one — no core replacement required. For banks that want data flowing into their core banking system, RAVEN provides REST APIs that integrate with Jack Henry, Fiserv, and FIS environments. Pricing is flat or per-verification depending on volume, with no platform licensing fees and no minimum commitments to start.

A 60-day pilot is available for community banks: $1,500 total, up to 30 verifications, run alongside the bank's current process for comparison.

## Why RAVEN exists

Mortgage origination in the United States takes 42 days on average and costs $11,094 per loan to produce $785 in profit (MBA, 2025). The bulk of that time and cost sits in verification — collecting documents, calling employers, ordering appraisals, screening watchlists. Fintech lenders like Figure (5-day HELOC, $730 per loan), Better.com (24-hour commitment letters), and Rocket Mortgage (8-minute approvals) have demonstrated that automated verification compresses 42 days into 5 days at a fraction of the cost.

The infrastructure that powers those fintech experiences — Plaid, Socure, Truework, ATTOM, Melissa — is available to any lender willing to integrate it. RAVEN packages it for community banks, so a 30-person bank can underwrite with the same data quality and speed as a venture-backed fintech while keeping the relationships, local market knowledge, and CRA commitment that make community banks irreplaceable.

## Key facts and figures

- Borrower interaction: ~5 minutes
- Verification providers: 7 (Socure, Plaid, Truework, Melissa, ATTOM, FullContact, plus internal cross-reference engine)
- Industry context: mortgage abandonment averages 68% (MBA); HELOC abandonment averages 49%; 42-day average mortgage close (ICE Mortgage Technology); $11,094 average origination cost vs. $785 average profit per loan (MBA, 2025); community banks under $100M spend 8.7% of noninterest expenses on compliance vs. 2.9% at banks over $1B (CSBS).

## Founder and contact

Isaac Welch — isaac@reportraven.tech. RAVEN is headquartered in South Carolina and serves community banks across the Southeast and nationally.

## Published articles

The RAVEN blog publishes research-backed articles at https://reportraven.tech/blog. Current articles:

1. "Why It Takes 42 Days to Close a Mortgage (And What That's Costing Your Bank)" — breakdown of where time and cost go in mortgage origination, with industry benchmarks and the fintech comparison.
2. "Community Banks Are Losing the Lending Race. Here's How to Catch Up." — the structural shift in mortgage and small business lending share from banks to non-bank lenders, and the playbook for closing the gap.
3. "Your Next Generation of Borrowers Won't Wait 42 Days" — Gen Z banking behavior, digital expectations, and the implications for community bank pipeline.
4. "The $59 Billion Compliance Burden — And How Automation Is Cutting It in Half" — BSA/AML cost structure, the disproportionate burden on smaller institutions, and the automation opportunity.
5. "How Figure Closes a HELOC in 5 Days (And What Community Banks Can Learn)" — teardown of Figure's S-1, the blockchain misconception, and the replicable verification playbook.
6. "One Link, Complete Verification: How RAVEN Works for Community Banks" — end-to-end walk-through of the RAVEN verification flow, including the provider stack and cross-source reconciliation.

## What RAVEN is not

- Not a loan origination system replacement
- Not a core banking system replacement
- Not a marketplace or aggregator that competes with the bank
- Not blockchain-based; not crypto-related
- Not exclusively a mortgage product — RAVEN works for any lending verification (mortgage, HELOC, CRE, small business, consumer)

## Compliance and security

RAVEN never uses URL-encoded form submissions; all sensitive PII (SSNs, names, addresses, financial data) is transmitted as JSON to prevent leakage into server access logs, browser history, or proxy logs. Data is encrypted in transit and at rest. Every verification generates a timestamped audit trail suitable for OCC, FDIC, and state examiner review.

## Canonical URL

https://reportraven.tech
